title: Low-Carb Friendly Canadian Roast Roast
description: A savory, umami-rich roast dish infused with the warmth of herbs and the subtle tang of sumac, perfect for a low-carb brunch. This recipe is a twist on traditional Canadian roast beef, with a focus on rich flavors and tender texture.
instructions: Season the beef roast with salt, black pepper, and sumac, if using.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the roast on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the roast to a roasting pan and add the minced garlic, thyme, and rosemary to the pan.
Baste the roast with beef broth and red wine, if using, and cover the pan with foil.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 2 hours, or until the roast reaches your desired level of doneness.
Let the roast rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the roast against the grain and serve hot.
Strain the juices from the pan and serve alongside the sliced roast.
